author: caezium
description: This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff. Control Spotify from your notes. Now-playing sidebar with hover controls, time-synced lyrics via LRCLIB, queue, search, transport hotkeys, and insert-track-into-note.

name: Spotify Control

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
First release built and published via the new GitHub Actions release workflow.

## Changes

- `main.js` and `styles.css` are now built from source on a clean runner and published with [GitHub artifact attestations](https://github.com/caezium/obsidian-spotify-control/attestations), cryptographically linking the binaries to this source commit via sigstore. Clears the attestation Recommendation from the Obsidian plugin-directory review.

No user-facing behavior changes from 0.5.4.

releases.author: caezium
releases.description:
Fixes remaining plugin directory review feedback from v0.5.3 submission.

## Changes

- Trim manifest description from 256 to 224 characters (directory limit is 250)
- Drop the standardized `scrollbar-width` / `scrollbar-color` declarations entirely; rely solely on `::-webkit-scrollbar` pseudo-elements for Obsidian's Chromium runtime. The CSS lint still flagged the properties even when gated behind `@supports`.

No user-facing behavior changes.

releases.author: caezium
releases.description:
Fixes Obsidian plugin directory review feedback from v0.5.1 submission.

## Changes

- Removed "Obsidian" from manifest description (redundant in directory context)
- Pointed `authorUrl` to personal GitHub profile instead of the plugin repo
- Replaced `builtin-modules` dependency with Node's built-in `module.builtinModules`
- Gated `scrollbar-width` / `scrollbar-color` behind `@supports` (only partially supported by Obsidian 1.4.5)
- Removed `!important` on `.sc-hide-in-hover-mode` in favor of double-class specificity

No user-facing behavior changes.

releases.author: caezium
releases.description:
Patch release with theme polish and clearer docs.

## Highlights

- **Theme-friendly colors.** Off-art elements (album art rim/shadow, lyrics + queue panel shadows, device dropdown, play button, device-active glow) now use Obsidian's `--shadow-s`, `--shadow-l`, `--background-modifier-border`, and `--color-green` instead of hardcoded `rgba()` values. Both light and dark themes now render correctly — no more invisible rims on light themes or invisible shadows on dark themes.
- **Premium clarification.** The plugin works on Spotify Free for display features (now-playing, lyrics, queue, search, insert-into-note). Premium is only required for the playback writes (play, pause, skip, seek, shuffle, repeat, volume, transfer) — that's a Spotify Web API restriction, not a plugin choice. Free users get a clear "Premium required" Notice when they try restricted actions, not silent failure.
- **README showcase.** Grid of screenshots up top showing the sidebar variants.

## Install (manual)

Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css` from the assets below and drop them into:

```
.obsidian/plugins/spotify-control/
```

Then enable **Spotify Control** in Obsidian → Settings → Community plugins.

Setup walkthrough lives in the plugin's settings tab (paste a Client ID from the Spotify Developer Dashboard, click Log in).

## Diff from v0.5.1

- 1 file changed (styles.css): theme-aware vars throughout
- Docs (README + manifest description) updated to reflect Free vs Premium accurately
- No behavioral or API changes

See [README.md](https://github.com/caezium/obsidian-spotify-control#readme) for the full feature list.

**Spotify Free** — display features work
**Spotify Premium** — playback control works (in addition to everything Free has)
releases.name: v0.5.2 — theme-friendly colors + docs polish

releases.author: caezium
releases.description:
Initial public release of Spotify Control.

## Install

**Manual install** (community plugin store submission pending):

1. Download \`main.js\`, \`manifest.json\`, \`styles.css\` from the assets below.
2. Drop them into \`.obsidian/plugins/spotify-control/\` in your vault.
3. In Obsidian → Settings → Community plugins → enable **Spotify Control**.
4. Follow the in-settings setup to register a Spotify Developer app + log in.

## Features

- Now-playing sidebar with hover-revealed transport (prev/play/next/shuffle/repeat on album art)
- Time-synced lyrics via [LRCLIB](https://lrclib.net) — click any line to seek
- Upcoming-tracks queue — click any track to jump while preserving playlist context
- Spotify search modal
- 10+ hotkey-bindable commands
- Insert now-playing into note (configurable template)
- Optional progress bar / volume button directly on the album art

## Spotify account tier

- **Free**: display features work (now-playing, lyrics, queue, search, insert-into-note)
- **Premium**: required for playback control (play/pause/skip/seek/shuffle/repeat/volume) — a Spotify Web API restriction. Free users get a clear "Premium required" message when they try restricted actions.

## Requires

- A Spotify Developer app (free, takes ~2 minutes to set up — instructions in the plugin settings)

See [README.md](https://github.com/caezium/obsidian-spotify-control#readme) for full setup and architecture notes.
releases.name: v0.5.1 — initial release
